steps.title: Measure years from the start
steps.content: Let $t$ be the number of years after $1996$. Then $1996$ is $t=0$, $2016$ is $t=20$, and $2020$ is $t=24$. The model gives $61$ thousand people when $t=0$ and $225$ thousand people when $t=20$.

steps.title: Find the yearly change
steps.content:
The slope of the linear model is
$$\frac{225-61}{20-0}=\frac{164}{20}=8.2$$
Since the value at $t=0$ is $61$, the model is
$$y=8.2t+61$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Evaluate the model at $t=24$. $$8.2(24)+61=257.8$$ Rounded to the nearest whole number, this is $258$. The answer is 258.
